children.heading: .—Limitation on Reporting in Miscellaneous Services Object Class
children.label: a .—Limitation on Reporting in Miscellaneous Services Object Class
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that, in reporting to the Office of Management and Budget (pursuant to OMB Circular A–11 (relating to preparation and submission of budget estimates)) obligations of the Department of Defense for any period of time for contract services, no more than 15 percent of the total amount of obligations so reported is reported in the miscellaneous services object class.

children.heading: .—Definition of Reporting Categories for Advisory and Assistance Services
children.label: b .—Definition of Reporting Categories for Advisory and Assistance Services
A
children.text: are closely related to the basic responsibilities and mission of the using organization; and

children.label: B
children.text: include efforts that support or contribute to improved organization or program management, logistics management, project monitoring and reporting, data collection, budgeting, accounting, auditing, and administrative or technical support for conferences and training programs.

children.heading: .—Studies, analyses, and evaluations
children.label: 2 .—Studies, analyses, and evaluations
children.text: The term “studies, analyses, and evaluations” (used in clause (ii) of ) means services that provide organized, analytic assessments to understand or evaluate complex issues to improve policy development, decisionmaking, management, or administration and that result in documents containing data or leading to conclusions or recommendations. Those services may include databases, models, methodologies, and related software created in support of a study, analysis, or evaluation.section 1105(g)(2)(A) of title 31

children.heading: .—Engineering and technical services
children.label: 3 .—Engineering and technical services
children.text: The term “engineering and technical services” (used in clause (iii) of ) means services that take the form of advice, assistance, training, or hands-on training necessary to maintain and operate fielded weapon systems, equipment, and components (including software when applicable) at design or required levels of effectiveness.section 1105(g)(2)(A) of title 31

children.heading: .—Proper Classification of Advisory and Assistance Services
children.label: c .—Proper Classification of Advisory and Assistance Services
children.text: Before the submission to the Office of Management and Budget of the proposed Department of Defense budget for inclusion in the President’s budget for a fiscal year pursuant to , the Secretary of Defense, acting through the Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller), shall conduct a review of Department of Defense services expected to be performed as contract services during the fiscal year for which that budget is to be submitted in order to ensure that those services that are advisory and assistance services (as defined in accordance with subsection (b)) are in fact properly classified, in accordance with that subsection, in the advisory and assistance services object class.section 1105 of title 31

children.heading: .—Report to Congress
children.label: d .—Report to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to Congress each year, not later than 30 days after the date on which the budget for the next fiscal year is submitted pursuant to , a report containing the information derived from the review under subsection (c).section 1105 of title 31

children.label: A
children.text: assess the methodology used by the Secretary in obtaining the information submitted to Congress in that report; and

children.label: B
children.text: assess the information submitted to Congress in that report.

Not later than 120 days after the date on which the Secretary submits to Congress the report required under subsection (d) for any year, the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress the Comptroller General’s report containing the results of the review for that year under paragraph (1).

The term “contract services” means all services that are reported to the Office of Management and Budget pursuant to OMB Circular A–11 (relating to preparation and submission of budget estimates) in budget object classes that are designated in the Object Class 25 series.

The term “advisory and assistance services object class” means those contract services constituting the budget object class that is denominated “Advisory and Assistance Service” and designated (as of ) as Object Class 25.1 (or any similar object class established after , for the reporting of obligations for advisory and assistance contract services).October 17, 1998October 17, 1998

The term “miscellaneous services object class” means those contract services constituting the budget object class that is denominated “Other Services (services not otherwise specified in the 25 series)” and designated (as of ) as Object Class 25.2 (or any similar object class established after , for the reporting of obligations for miscellaneous or unspecified contract services).October 17, 1998October 17, 1998

The term “authorized exemptions” means those exemptions authorized (as of ) under Department of Defense Directive 4205.2, captioned “Acquiring and Managing Contracted Advisory and Assistance Services (CAAS)” and issued by the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Technology on , such exemptions being set forth in Enclosure 3 to that directive (captioned “CAAS Exemptions”).October 17, 1998February 10, 1992
